content.summaryText: Viral vs Bacterial Patterns in Respiratory Infections focuses on triage severity and supportive respiratory care. Primary objective: Recognize symptom patterns.

content.questionText: In Viral vs Bacterial Patterns, which action best supports 'Recognize symptom patterns'?
content.explanation: Correct answers prioritize patient safety, reassessment, and timely escalation.

options.text: Assess respiratory rate, oxygenation, and danger symptoms
Underestimating deterioration in older or comorbid patients
Delay reassessment until symptoms are severe
Use treatment decisions without baseline documentation

content.summaryText: Immediate priority: assess respiratory rate, oxygenation, and danger symptoms. Practical emphasis: Interpret basic inflammatory clues.

content.questionText: Escalation is required when hypoxia, work of breathing increase, hemodynamic instability.
content.explanation: Early escalation reduces preventable deterioration and delays in definitive care.

content.sentence:
Start with <1> to establish baseline status.
Use <2> to deliver standardized intervention.
Document <3> to support continuity and quality review.
